title: The Louvre
description: The official website is the best option (about 20€). If it's fully booked, some operators sell guided visits with reserved access.

title: Musée d'Orsay ⭐
description: Book official timed-entry tickets first. If sold out, the guided Impressionism tour (ticket included) is a great alternative.

title: 14 Paris city museums are free ⭐
description: The City of Paris runs 14 museums with permanent collection free of charge! Including Petit Palais, Musée Carnavalet, House of Victor Hugo!. No booking needed.

title: Palace of Versailles
description: 40 minutes from Paris by RER C. A full day out. You can go on your own with official tickets, or book a guided visit to really make sense of it all.

title: Eiffel Tower
description: Book directly on the official website. Also, some tours are offered, sometimes not the best ones :(

title: Other art museums
description: Petit Palais (free!) / Musée de l'Orangerie / Musée de Cluny (free under 26)

title: Musée Rodin
description: Intimate museum with a lovely garden, just opposite the Invalides. Much calmer than the Louvre.

title: Unusual museums
description: Musée de la Préfecture de Police (Police Museum) / Musée des Égouts (Sewer Museum) / Musée du Fromage (Cheese Museum)

title: Père Lachaise Cemetery Tour
description: A tour through the graves of Édith Piaf, Jim Morrison, and Oscar Wilde. More stories than you can imagine!

title: Montmartre Hill Tour
description: The bohemian village above Paris.. Renoir, Picasso, the Moulin Rouge, and the best view of the city.

title: Seine River Cruise — Bateaux Mouches
description: The classic Paris experience. 1h10 along the river, past Notre-Dame, the Eiffel Tower, and the Louvre.

title: Amphibious Bus — road + river in one
description: A bus that becomes a boat halfway through. Unusual, fun for kids and adults alike. Covers the main sights then hits the Seine.

title: Mont Saint-Michel
description: The most visited site in France outside Paris. Train + shuttle from Paris takes ~4h.

title: Champagne region — Reims or Épernay
description: 45 minutes by TGV to Reims or Épernay. Cellar tours, tasting rooms, and the magnificent Reims Cathedral. A brilliant day out.

title: How to use the Paris Métro
description: Get a Navigo Easy card (€2). Use a day pass if you travel a lot. Otherwise, note that metro/RER and bus/tram tickets are separate, so one ticket won’t work for both.

title: CDG Airport to Paris centre
description: RER B is the main option (€14, ~35–45 min to Châtelet). Uber/Bolt can be cheaper if you are 3+ people. Cheapest option: bus + metro (~€2.50 but much longer).

title: Orly Airport to Paris centre
description: Metro line 14 is the easiest (€14, ~25 min). Uber/Bolt can be cheaper if you are 3+ people. Note: regular metro tickets don’t work for the airport.

title: Hop-on Hop-off Bus
description: A relaxed way to cover the major sights in one day.
